steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The strongest clue in the passage is the phrase ‘I did what my teacher had advised. Pretend you’re only playing for one person. That way you won’t be so nervous.’ This directly explains the narrator’s mental strategy as she walks to the piano.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Choice D captures the shift exactly. It references the narrator’s adherence to her teacher’s advice to manage nervousness, which aligns with the explicit instruction and outcome described. A, B, and C each miss the contrast—A misstates the focus as practice vs. performance, B conflates nervousness with excitement, and C introduces an unstated detail. That matches choice D — D is correct.
